summary: Verified new axios response-retry interceptor did NOT regress normal auth or API flows and 401 login still fails fast (not retried). Backend pytest: 28/28 passed. Frontend: (a) invalid password error 'Invalid email or password' surfaced in 0.39s (well under the 5s threshold, confirming 4xx are excluded from the retry loop); (b) password eye toggle switches auth-password-input between type='password' and type='text' and back; (c) admin login admin@lottopredict.app/admin123 lands on /dashboard in 0.50s with lotto_token stored (len=215); (d) signup fresh user test_ret_<ts>@lottopredict.app -> /dashboard 0.50s; (e) logout clears token; (f) re-login same creds -> /dashboard 0.83s with zero auth-error nodes; (g) Statistical predict returned 3 sets; save-prediction-0 -> item appeared on /saved; delete-saved-<id> -> 'Removed' toast, list empty; (h) dashboard renders Hot/Cold/Overdue and Number Frequency chart.
critical_code_review_comments: api.js retry interceptor is well-scoped: only retries when !error.response (network/timeout) OR status in [502,503,504]; 4xx (incl 401) fall straight through -> confirmed empirically (401 surfaced in 0.39s).
api.js: DELETE requests are correctly excluded from retry (config.method === 'delete') so idempotency concerns on non-idempotent-ish deletes are handled.

Minor consideration: retry decision uses `!error.response` which also treats client-side errors (e.g. cancel/aborted, request setup issues) as transient. In practice these are rare in this app, but if AbortController is added later, consider also short-circuiting on error.code === 'ERR_CANCELED'.

Minor: MAX_RETRIES=4 with 2.5s delay = up to 10s user-perceived wait on a fully-dead backend. That is intentional per the fix rationale (cold-start), but a subtle loading indicator hint like 'Waking server…' after the first retry would improve UX. Not blocking.

Pre-existing (not in scope): backend_test.py line 12 still keeps a hardcoded fallback URL; CORS_ORIGINS='*' + allow_credentials=True remains a spec violation but Bearer flow works. Both were flagged in prior iterations.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (regression + 401 fast-fail + signup/logout/re-login + statistical save/delete all pass)

test_credentials: admin@lottopredict.app / admin123 verified; fresh signup user test_ret_1783974624@lottopredict.app created and re-authenticated successfully.
seed_data_creation: One signup user created (test_ret_1783974624@lottopredict.app); saved-prediction created and then deleted during the flow, so no residual test data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Retry interceptor in /app/frontend/src/lib/api.js: retries only network errors and 502/503/504 up to 4x with 2.5s delay; 4xx (incl 401) fall through immediately, DELETE not retried. Verified 401 fast-fail at 0.39s. All prior data-testids stable: auth-name-input, auth-email-input, auth-password-input, toggle-password-visibility, auth-submit, auth-error, auth-toggle-mode, nav-logout, generate-statistical, prediction-set-<i>, save-prediction-<i>, saved-item-<id>, delete-saved-<id>. Backend BASE_URL for tests: https://jackpot-analyzer-24.preview.emergentagent.com.
rca of the issue: N/A - this iteration was a regression pass on the retry interceptor. Confirmed the fix works as designed: transient 502/503/504/network failures self-heal (bounded by 4 retries × 2.5s), while genuine 4xx (401 for wrong password) short-circuits in <1s so users still get immediate feedback. Original 'Can't reach the server' report was preview idle cold-start, and the interceptor now masks that class of failure end-to-end.
